sections.content:
Now I see: at [18, 48], the blue square [15, 47] (center [16, 48]) is covered by the vertical bar and disappeared. 3 blue squares remain: [23, 39], [23, 52], [34, 47].
So the pattern is confirmed: squares disappear when covered by the cross bars and reappear when the cross moves away. The only permanently collected square is [8, 5] (yellow).
I need to understand what made [8, 5] permanently collected. Let me think about this differently. Maybe the goal isn't to collect squares at all. Maybe the goal is to position both crosses such that all squares of their matching color are simultaneously covered.
For blue squares: [16, 48], [24, 40], [24, 53], [35, 48]
- Can one cross position cover all 4? No, they're too spread out.
For yellow squares: [3, 15], [9, 6], [9, 24], [17, 15] (centers)
- [3, 15] and [17, 15] share col 15
- [9, 6] and [9, 24] share row 9
- If cross center is at [9, 15]: vertical bar col 15, rows 3-15; horizontal bar row 9, cols 9-21
  - [3, 15]: ON vertical bar ✓
  - [17, 15]: row 17 > 15, NOT covered ✗
  - [9, 6]: row 9, col 6 < 9, NOT covered ✗
  - [9, 24]: row 9, col 24 > 21, NOT covered ✗
Hmm, that doesn't work either. Let me try [10, 15]:
- Vertical: col 15, rows 4-16
- Horizontal: row 10, cols 9-21
- [3, 15]: row 3 < 4, NOT covered
- [17, 15]: row 17 > 16, NOT covered
- [9, 6]: row 9 ≠ 10, col 6 ≠ 15, NOT covered
- [9, 24]: NOT covered
What about [9, 15]?
- Vertical: col 15, rows 3-15
- Horizontal: row 9, cols 9-21
- [3, 15]: row 3 in [3, 15], col 15 = 15 ✓
- [17, 15]: row 17 > 15 ✗
- [9, 6]: row 9 = 9, col 6 < 9 ✗
- [9, 24]: row 9 = 9, col 24 > 21 ✗
Still only 1 out of 4. Maybe the goal is to cover as many as possible?
Actually, wait. Let me reconsider the whole game. Maybe the goal is NOT about collecting squares at all. Maybe it's about something else entirely. Let me look at the bottom bar more carefully - it has purple and green sections. Maybe that represents something I need to match or complete.
Also, I notice the yellow cross is at [27, 21] (its initial position) and hasn't moved since I switched back to blue. Maybe I need to position BOTH crosses in specific locations.
Let me try a completely different approach: what if I need to align the crosses so that their bars form a pattern or connect to each other? Or what if I need to position the crosses so that ALL squares (regardless of color) are covered by at least one cross?
Actually, let me reconsider the [8, 5] collection. Looking back at the history:
- I moved the yellow cross from [27, 21] to [18, 15] (UP 3, LEFT 2)
- At [18, 15], the cross covers rows 12-24 at col 15 and row 18 at cols 9-21
- The square [8, 5] has center [9, 6]
- [9, 6] is NOT on any bar
- But the square disappeared
Wait, maybe I was wrong about which square disappeared. Let me check the history more carefully. Actually, I can't easily trace back that far. Let me just focus on what I know now:
1. Squares disappear when covered by cross bars
2. Squares reappear when the cross moves away
3. One yellow square [8, 5] seems permanently collected
4. The bottom bar has purple and green sections
Let me try yet another approach: what if the goal is to make ALL squares disappear simultaneously? I'd need to position both crosses to cover all 7 remaining squares (3 blue + 3 yellow, since [8,5] is gone).
Blue: [23, 39], [23, 52], [34, 47] (centers: [24, 40], [24, 53], [35, 48])
Yellow: [2, 14], [8, 23], [16, 14] (centers: [3, 15], [9, 24], [17, 15])
For yellow cross to cover all 3 yellow squares:
- [3, 15] and [17, 15] share col 15
- [9, 24] is on row 9, col 24
- If yellow cross at [10, 15]: vertical col 15, rows 4-16; horizontal row 10, cols 9-21
  - [3, 15]: row 3 < 4 ✗
  - [17, 15]: row 17 > 16 ✗
  - [9, 24]: row 9 ≠ 10, col 24 > 21 ✗
What about [10, 24]?
- Vertical: col 24, rows 4-16
- Horizontal: row 10, cols 18-30
- [3, 15]: ✗
- [17, 15]: ✗
- [9, 24]: row 9 ≠ 10, col 24 = 24 ✓ (on vertical bar? row 9 in [4, 16], col 24 = 24) ✓
What about [10, 19]?
- Vertical: col 19, rows 4-16
- Horizontal: row 10, cols 13-25
- [3, 15]: ✗
- [17, 15]: ✗
- [9, 24]: row 9 ≠ 10, col 24 ≠ 19 ✗
Hmm, I can't cover all 3 yellow squares with one cross. What if I use both crosses?
- Yellow cross covers some yellow squares
- Blue cross covers remaining yellow squares + blue squares
Actually wait, maybe the blue cross can cover yellow squares too? Let me check: when the blue cross was at [24, 39], did any yellow squares disappear? No, all yellow squares remained. So crosses only cover squares of their own color.
So the goal might be: position each cross to cover ALL squares of its color. But as I showed, this seems geometrically impossible for the current layout.
Unless... the cross bars are wider than I think. Let me check the actual bar dimensions from segmentation.
Actually, I just realized something. Let me look at the bottom bar more carefully. It has a purple section on the left and a green section on the right. Maybe this represents the two crosses' "progress" - how many squares of each color have been collected. If purple = yellow cross progress and green = blue cross progress, then the sizes might indicate how many squares are covered.
